function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
Is there a call path from function 'main' to function 'printf'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i < argc' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i < argc' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < num_states' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'i < num_states' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 20:  <statement> states[i] = argv[i]; </statement> is reached, the statement line 28:  <statement> printf("newarray: %d: %s\n", i, newarray[i]); </statement> has already been reached?
Does the statement line 5:  <statement> while (i >= 0) { </statement> dominate line 32:  <statement> printf("newarray: %d: %s\n", i, newarray[i]); </statement>?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable i at line 7: <statement>         i--; </statement> depend on the value of variable i at line 4: <statement>     int i = (argc - 1); </statement>?

Does the value of variable i at line 23: <statement>     for (i = 0; i < num_states; i++) { </statement> depend on the value of variable argc at line 2: <statement> int main(int argc, char *argv[]) </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'states' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'argc' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the statement '};' be considered dead code?
Can the program actually reach the statement: “i--;”?
